questions.q: What is the output of: print(type([]))?
Which of the following is an immutable data type in Python?
What does the 'self' parameter represent in a class method?
What is the output of: print(2 ** 10)?
Which method is used to add an element to the end of a list?
What is a lambda function in Python?
options: A named function with multiple lines
An anonymous function defined with a single expression

questions.q: What does the 'pass' statement do?
options: Acts as a placeholder doing nothing
Passes a value to a function

questions.q: What is the output of: print(bool(''))?
Which of the following is used to handle exceptions in Python?
What is a decorator in Python?
options: A way to style print output
A function that wraps another function to extend its behavior

questions.q: What is the output of: print(list(range(1, 6)))?
What is the difference between '==' and 'is' in Python?
options: '==' checks value equality; 'is' checks identity (same object)

questions.q: What does the zip() function do?
options: Combines multiple iterables element-by-element into tuples

questions.q: What is a list comprehension?
options: A concise way to create lists using a single line expression with an optional condition

questions.q: What is the output of: print('hello'[::-1])?
Which keyword is used to create a generator function?
What is the output of: print(type(None))?
What does *args do in a function definition?
options: Accepts any number of positional arguments as a tuple

questions.q: What is the output of: print(10 // 3)?
Which built-in function returns the largest item in an iterable?
What is a Python module?
options: A file containing Python definitions and statements that can be imported

questions.q: What does **kwargs allow in a function?
options: Accepts any number of keyword arguments as a dictionary

questions.q: What is the output of: print([i**2 for i in range(4)])?
What is the purpose of __init__ in a Python class?
options: Initializes the object's attributes when an instance is created

questions.q: What does the enumerate() function return?
options: An enumerate object with index-value pairs

questions.q: What is the output of: print(set([1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3]))?
What is shallow copy vs deep copy?
options: Shallow copy copies references; deep copy creates independent copies of nested objects
Shallow copy only works on tuples

questions.q: Which method returns all keys in a dictionary?
What is the output of: print('Python' in 'I love Python')?
What does the map() function do?
options: Applies a function to every item in an iterable and returns a map object

questions.q: What is the output of: print(bool(0))?
What is the purpose of the 'finally' block in exception handling?
options: Runs only if no exception occurs
Runs only if an exception occurs
Always runs regardless of whether an exception occurred

questions.q: What is method overriding in Python?
options: Defining multiple methods with the same name in one class
A child class redefining a method from the parent class

questions.q: What does the filter() function return?
options: An iterator of elements for which the function returns True

questions.q: What is the output of: print(len({1: 'a', 2: 'b', 3: 'c'}))?
What is a context manager in Python?
options: A way to manage memory manually
An object that sets up and tears down a context using __enter__ and __exit__

questions.q: What is the output of: print(type((1,)))?
What does 'global' keyword do inside a function?
options: Allows the function to modify a variable defined in the global scope

questions.q: What is the output of: print(sorted([3, 1, 4, 1, 5], reverse=True))?
What is multiple inheritance in Python?
options: A class that inherits from more than one parent class
Inheriting the same class multiple times

questions.q: What is the output of: print(10 % 3)?
What does the @staticmethod decorator do?
options: Defines a method that modifies class state
Defines a method that belongs to the class but doesn't receive class or instance as first argument

questions.q: What is the output of: print(''.join(['a', 'b', 'c']))?
What is pickling in Python?
options: Serializing a Python object into a byte stream for storage or transmission

questions.q: What does the 'nonlocal' keyword do?
options: Allows a nested function to modify a variable from the enclosing (non-global) scope
Prevents variable access outside the function

questions.q: What is the output of: print(any([False, False, True]))?
What is duck typing in Python?
options: Checking an object's type before use
An approach where the type of object matters less than whether it supports the required methods
A way to convert types automatically

questions.q: What does __str__ method do in a class?
options: Converts the class to a dictionary
Defines the string representation returned by str() or print()

questions.q: What is the output of: print(all([True, True, False]))?
What is the GIL (Global Interpreter Lock) in Python?
options: A security feature for Python packages
A mutex that allows only one thread to execute Python bytecode at a time
A way to lock files during I/O
